element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Available from game start; foundation for all combat
Paired with attack button for mobility and evasion
Unlocked by pressing both buttons; limited by energy cost
Available to Fighter, Cleric, Dwarf; requires directional input before impact
Enemies drop experience; level-ups increase health, range, attributes; grants temporary invulnerability
Converts to experience points; incentivizes defeat of all enemies
Three instances hidden in game; high-value discovery
Punctuate level sequences; require combat mastery
Mid-game difficulty spike; multi-part boss with cinematic defeat
Campaign objective; defeated after 16 levels of progression

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Short levels interspersed with boss encounters; frequent opportunity for relief via leveling system; RPG mechanics create periodic power-spike relief cycles overlaid on continuous beat-em-up action tension

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Hidden yashichi power-ups require exploration; placement not specified in sources

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible; single attack button and basic jump establish foundation; early levels allow leveling before major threats
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Hydra boss at Level 4 represents documented difficulty spike; character attributes have improved but enemy complexity increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Escalating toward final dragon Gildiss encounter; 16 levels total suggests steady progression with boss punctuation
notable_spikes: Hydra boss (Level 4) - multi-part enemy requiring combat mastery

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level advances toward an objective zone; many levels are explicitly described as short; boss encounter concludes each segment
Each of five character types (Fighter, Cleric, Dwarf, Elf, Wizard) has distinct attributes (melee, magic, range, defense, agility) validated through level design requiring different approaches
notable_level_patterns.example_level: All levels - design accommodates varied playstyles

notable_level_patterns.description: Magic attacks deplete energy; shield defense unavailable to some classes; temporary invulnerability post-level-up creates windows of safety that must be leveraged strategically

sources_used: Wikipedia - The King of Dragons (gameplay section)
The Cutting Room Floor - The King of Dragons (SNES) (regional differences context)
